name: Panama (Republic of Panama / República de Panamá)
summary: Panama is a Central American civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code 1994 (Código de la Familia, Law 3 of 1994), drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Parental authority (patria potestad) and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 308-359. The Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Courts (Juzgados Seccionales de Familia). Psychology profession is regulated under the Colegio Nacional de Psicólogos and Ministerio de Salud licensing framework. Panama is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Family Code art. 481. Panama acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 1994 — among the earliest Latin American accessions in the corpus.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Arts. 308-359 govern patria potestad and child custody.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio Nacional de Psicólogos de Panamá
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional college for psychologists in Panama.

anonymisation_convention: Panamanian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Panama independence from Colombia + US Canal Treaty + foundational framework
key_developments.description: Panama declared independence 3 November 1903 from Colombia with US support — substantively distinctive US-facilitated independence framework. Hay-Bunau-Varilla Treaty 18 November 1903 establishing Panama Canal Zone US-jurisdiction-enclave. Foundational US-Panama dual-sovereignty-arrangement persisting through 1999.

key_developments.title: Constitution 1972 + Torrijos military-populist framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Panama adopted 11 October 1972 (substantially amended 1978, 1983, 1994, 2004) — establishing presidential republic framework under Omar Torrijos military-populist framework. Substantively distinctive military-dominant political-institutional framework persisting through 1989.

key_developments.title: Torrijos-Carter Treaties + Canal sovereignty transfer trajectory
key_developments.description: Torrijos-Carter Treaties signed 7 September 1977 — establishing 31 December 1999 Canal sovereignty transfer to Panama. Substantively distinctive sovereignty-restoration trajectory affecting cross-border-jurisdiction practice. Foundational framework for contemporary Panama-US relationship.

key_developments.title: US Operation Just Cause + Noriega arrest + democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: US Operation Just Cause 20 December 1989 — Manuel Noriega ousted and arrested. Substantively distinctive 20th-century US-Caribbean military-intervention pattern. Subsequent democratic-transition trajectory establishing contemporary constitutional-democratic framework affecting family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Panama ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Panama ratified the UNCRC on 12 December 1990 — among the early Latin American ratifications globally. Framing the family-law-reform trajectory toward best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within civil-law framework.

key_developments.title: Family Code 1994 (Law 3 of 1994) + Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 17 May 1994 (Law 3 of 1994) drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Arts. 308-359 govern patria potestad and child custody. Art. 481 codifies superior-interest-of-the-child standard. Panama acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 1994 — among the earliest Latin American accessions in the corpus.

key_developments.title: Canal sovereignty transfer + Mireya Moscoso presidency + millennium framework
key_developments.description: Panama Canal sovereignty transfer 31 December 1999 — substantively distinctive sovereignty-restoration completion. Mireya Moscoso first female president 1999-2004. Substantive shift in cross-border-jurisdiction practice with full sovereignty restoration affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Canal expansion + Cobre Panamá mining + extractive-economy framework
key_developments.description: Panama Canal expansion 26 June 2016 completion ($5.25 billion) substantively reshaping global maritime trade. Cobre Panamá copper mine opened 2019 — substantively significant extractive-economy framework subsequently affecting political contestation. Substantive Constitutional Court mining contract decision 2023.

key_developments.title: Panama Papers 2016 + offshore-financial-system reform + AML-CFT framework
key_developments.description: Panama Papers leak 3 April 2016 substantively exposed Mossack Fonseca offshore-financial-system. Substantial reform trajectory through 2016-2020s including AML-CFT compliance framework consolidation. Substantive financial-services-regulatory-framework affecting cross-border-family-law-asset-tracing.

key_developments.title: Cortizo presidency transition + Defensoría del Pueblo + SENNIAF framework
key_developments.description: Laurentino Cortizo presidency 2019-2024 framework continuing through 2023 under sustained institutional context. Defensoría del Pueblo and SENNIAF (Secretaría Nacional de Niñez, Adolescencia y Familia) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Family Code framework. Multiple Corte Suprema decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation establishing trajectory toward 2024 Mulino presidency framework. Substantively significant Central American institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Corte Suprema de Justicia — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Mulino presidency
key_developments.description: Corte Suprema de Justicia continues to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code art. 481 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes without adopting the 'parental alienation' label as a doctrinal term. José Raúl Mulino elected May 2024 — substantively significant Martinelli-aligned-candidate-replacement framework. Substantive analysis within civil-law + Hague Convention framework.

structural_findings: Panama operates a Spanish-civil-law family-law framework — places Panama in the Central American civil-law cluster with Guatemala and Costa Rica.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 1994 places Panama as among the earliest Latin American Hague accessions within the corpus.
Family Code 1994 simultaneous codification + Hague accession represents structurally distinctive modernisation moment.
US-Panama dual-sovereignty-arrangement 1903-1999 + Canal sovereignty restoration 1999 structurally distinctive among Central American states.
Panama Papers 2016 offshore-financial-system disclosure substantively reshaped global AML-CFT compliance framework — structurally significant for cross-border-family-law-asset-tracing.
editorial_notes: Panama jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 1 to 10 key_developments with full 1903-independence-to-Mulino trajectory: 1903-Panama-independence-from-Colombia-+-US-Canal-Treaty + 1972-Constitution-+-Torrijos-military-populist + 1977-Torrijos-Carter-Treaties + 1989-US-Operation-Just-Cause-+-Noriega-arrest + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1994-Family-Code-Law-3-+-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 1999-Canal-sovereignty-transfer-+-Moscoso-female-president + 2014-2016-Canal-expansion-+-Cobre-Panamá-mining + 2016-Panama-Papers-+-AML-CFT-framework + 2024-Corte-Suprema-de-Justicia-superior-interest-of-the-child-+-Mulino-presidency.
Civil-law Central America (Family Code 1994 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 1994 — earliest Latin American).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code arts. 308-359 + art. 481 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Latin-American + civil-law + Central-American + US-Panama-dual-sovereignty-1903-1999-distinctive + Canal-sovereignty-restoration-1999 + US-Operation-Just-Cause-1989 + earliest-Hague-Convention-1994-Latin-American + Panama-Papers-2016-AML-CFT-distinctive + Canal-expansion-2016 + Mulino-Martinelli-aligned-2024 clusters within the corpus.
